notes: v1.14.7 — globe polish: even atmosphere rim + deeper sun / sharper terminator (builds on the v1.14.6 lit ocean sphere). Three surgical edits, all inside drawUnderlay() which runs on resize only — zero per-frame cost. PART A (even atmosphere rim): added a 4th and LAST canvas-2D fill — an even concentric fresnel rim (createRadialGradient CX/CY radial 0.90R->1.07R; the bright peak stop alpha 0.32 at offset 0.59 lands exactly at r=R) so the limb glows evenly ALL the way around, fixing the bottom-only edge (the .pl-horizon CSS lit only the bottom; left as-is). Drawn last so it overlays the body's dark limb and fades into space by 1.07R. PART B (deeper sun + sharper terminator): B1 pushed the sun point further off-center (LX/LY = CX/CY - 0.38R -> 0.48R); B2 compressed the directional-light falloff (lit gradient outer 1.35R->1.25R, added a 0.70-offset near-dark stop, nudged colors brighter at the core) so near limb 0.32R = lit, center 0.68R = moderate (stays lit, NOT a crescent), far limb 1.68R = shadow. The sheen rides the same LX/LY so the glint tracks the sun automatically. PART C (arc prominence) was HELD per the spec — it reopens the v1.7.6 additive-wash risk on the lit face and is its own separate cut. Real visual feature -> quad bump v1.14.6->v1.14.7 + cache bump. Verified: node --check 3/3 inline scripts + sw.js, block-0 CSS braces 2736/2736 (no CSS change — all edits are JS), CRLF preserved (0 lone LF), four stamps lockstep v1.14.7, sw.js cache-bumped, git diff surgical. Adversarially verified (4-lens workflow). NOT runtime-tested — canvas/WebGL can't render in the sandbox; John's device watch: (1) the limb glows evenly all the way around (not just the bottom) and the sphere reads more clearly lit-from-upper-left, (2) if the rim is too hot or too cyan the knob is the rim's 0.32 peak alpha + the rgba(140,232,255) hue, (3) crescent check — if the center went too dark with the deeper sun, ease LX/LY from 0.48 toward 0.42. WARNING: standing repo-only fixes — v1.13.8 (#boot full-bleed) and v1.14.4 (storage-full recovery) still lack a counterpart in the external/web-Claude build base; re-apply on the next import or they drop.
